front: Composer: French; best known for Carmen and The Pearl Fishers; his teacher was Charles Gounod
Composer: suppressed his Symphony in C because it resembled his teacher's Symphony in D; it was first performed 60 years after his death
Composer: Ernest Guiraud arranged his second L'Arlésienne suite and wrote orchestral recitatives for his opera Carmen
Opera: Zurga and Nadir sing the friendship duet 'Au fond du temple saint'; both are in love with the Brahman priestess Leila
work: The Pearl Fishers (Les pêcheurs de perles)

front: Opera: set in ancient Sri Lanka; Nadir sings 'Je crois entendre encore' admitting he betrayed Zurga; high priest Nourabad guards the priestess Leila
work: The Pearl Fishers (Les pêcheurs de perles)

front: Opera: Hector Berlioz gave it a glowing review in his final feuilleton
work: The Pearl Fishers (Les pêcheurs de perles)

front: Suite: two suites from incidental music for Alphonse Daudet's play; both feature the Epiphany hymn 'March of the Kings' — in the Prelude and the Farandole
Suite: Suite No. 1's 'Carillon' movement has French horns imitating church bells with the three-note ostinato G-sharp, E, F-sharp
Symphony: written at age 17 at the Paris Conservatory; first performed 60 years after his death; inspired Balanchine's ballet The Crystal Palace
Opera: Micaëla (not in the original Mérimée novella) sings 'Je dis que rien ne m'épouvante' in Act III; the Habanera melody was unknowingly taken from a Sebastián Iradier song
